name: backend-ai-common

metadata.name: backend.ai-common
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)

documentation:
Backend.AI Commons
==================

[![PyPI release version](https://badge.fury.io/py/backend.ai-common.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/backend.ai-common/)
![Supported Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/backend.ai-common.svg)
[![Gitter](https://badges.gitter.im/lablup/backend.ai-common.svg)](https://gitter.im/lablup/backend.ai-common)

Common utilities library for Backend.AI


## Installation

```console
$ pip install backend.ai-common
```

## For development

```console
$ pip install -U pip setuptools
$ pip install -U -r requirements/dev.txt
```

### Running test suite

```console
$ python -m pytest
```

With the default halfstack setup, you may need to set the environment variable `BACKEND_ETCD_ADDR`
to specify the non-standard etcd service port (e.g., `localhost:8110`).

The tests for `common.redis` module requires availability of local TCP ports 16379, 16380, 16381,
26379, 26380, and 26381 to launch a temporary Redis sentinel cluster via `docker compose`.

In macOS, they require a local `redis-server` executable to be installed, preferably via `brew`,
because `docker compose` in macOS does not support host-mode networking and Redis *cannot* be
configured to use different self IP addresses to announce to the cluster nodes and clients.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.utils.env_info
documentation: Returns a string that contains the Python version and runtime path.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.utils.odict
documentation:
A short-hand for the constructor of OrderedDict.
{code}`odict(('a',1), ('b',2))` is equivalent to
{code}`OrderedDict([('a',1), ('b',2)])`.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.utils.dict2kvlist
documentation:
Serializes a dict-like object into a generator of the flatten list of
repeating key-value pairs.  It is useful when using HMSET method in Redis.

Example:

   >>> list(dict2kvlist({'a': 1, 'b': 2}))
   ['a', 1, 'b', 2]

functions.name: ai.backend.common.utils.get_random_seq
documentation:
Generate a random sequence of numbers within the range [0, length]
with the given number of points and the minimum distance between the points.

Note that X ( = the minimum distance d x the number of points N) must be equivalent to or smaller than
the length L + d to guarantee the the minimum distance between the points.
If X == L + d, the points are always equally spaced with d.

```
return

An iterator over the generated sequence
```

functions.name: ai.backend.common.utils.nmget
documentation:
A short-hand for retrieving a value from nested mappings
("nested-mapping-get"). At each level it checks if the given "path"
component in the given key exists and return the default value whenever
fails.

Example:
\>>> o = {'a':{'b':1}, 'x': None}
\>>> nmget(o, 'a', 0)
{'b': 1}
\>>> nmget(o, 'a.b', 0)
1
\>>> nmget(o, 'a/b', 0, '/')
1
\>>> nmget(o, 'a.c', 0)
0
\>>> nmget(o, 'x', 0)
0
\>>> nmget(o, 'x', 0, null_as_default=False)
None

functions.name: ai.backend.common.utils.str_to_timedelta
documentation:
Convert humanized timedelta string into a Python timedelta object.

Example:
\>>> str_to_timedelta('30min')
datetime.timedelta(seconds=1800)
\>>> str_to_timedelta('1d1hr')
datetime.timedelta(days=1, seconds=3600)
\>>> str_to_timedelta('2hours 15min')
datetime.timedelta(seconds=8100)
\>>> str_to_timedelta('20sec')
datetime.timedelta(seconds=20)
\>>> str_to_timedelta('300')
datetime.timedelta(seconds=300)
\>>> str_to_timedelta('-1day')
datetime.timedelta(days=-1)

functions.name: ai.backend.common.utils.join_non_empty
documentation: Joins non-empty strings from the given arguments using the specified separator.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.utils.FstabEntry
documentation: Entry class represents a non-comment line on the `fstab` file.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.utils.Fstab
documentation:
Reader/writer for fstab file.
Takes aiofile pointer for async I/O. It should be writable if add/remove
operations are needed.

NOTE: This class references Jorge Niedbalski R.'s gist snippet.
      We have been converted it to be compatible with Python 3
      and to support async I/O.
      (https://gist.github.com/niedbalski/507e974ed2d54a87ad37)

functions.name: ai.backend.common.docker._search_docker_socket_files_impl
documentation: We may cache the connector type but not connector instances!

functions.name: ai.backend.common.docker.login
documentation:
Authorize to the docker registry using the given credentials and token scope, and returns a set
of required aiohttp.ClientSession.request() keyword arguments for further API requests.

Some registry servers only rely on HTTP Basic Authentication without token-based access controls
(usually via nginx proxy). We do support them also. :)

classes.name: ai.backend.common.docker.ImageRef

methods.name: ai.backend.common.docker.ImageRef.from_image_str
documentation: Parse the image reference string and return an ImageRef object from the string.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.docker.ImageRef.parse_image_tag
documentation:
Parses the image name and tag from the given image string.

When the `image_str` does not contain '/', and `using_default_repository` is True, it includes the `default_repository` (lablup) in the image.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.docker.ImageRef.parse_image_str
documentation:
Parses a string representing an image.

`image_str` basically follow the format below: `<registry>/<project>/<image name>:<version>-<tag>-<tag>...`
And if certain values are not provided (for example, if only the image name is given), hardcoded default values will be used.
Tags must begin with a letter and cannot end with a hyphen. And since hyphens are used to separate tags, a tag cannot contain a hyphen within itself.
For more details, you can refer to the `tests/common/test_docker.py`.

Here are some details about this function's behavior.
1. Passing '*' to `registry` parse any characters before the first '/' as the registry part.
2. Passing 'None' to `registry` use the default registry (`index.docker.io`).
   In this case, the `image_str` should be a combination of the project and image name without the registry part.
3. If the registry part of the `image_str` is in IP address format, it parses that value as the registry part regardless of the `registry` argument.
4. `ParsedImageStr` can not distinguish the project and the image name.
   If you already know the project value of the image, use `from_image_str()` instead of this function.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.docker.ImageRef.short
documentation: Returns the image reference string without the registry part.
Represent image reference.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.testutils.mock_corofunc
documentation:
Return mock coroutine function.

Python's default mock module does not support coroutines.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.testutils.mock_awaitable
documentation:
Mock awaitable.

An awaitable can be a native coroutine object "returned from" a native
coroutine function.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.testutils.AsyncContextManagerMock
documentation:
Mock async context manager.

Can be used to get around `async with` statement for testing.
Must implement `__aenter__` and `__aexit__` which returns awaitable.
Attributes of the awaitable (and self for convenience) can be set by
passing `kwargs`.

modules.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify
documentation:
A wrapper for systemd's daemon status notification protocol.
The methods will silently becomes no-op if NOTIFY_SOCKET environment variable is not set.

This module implements a subset of the notification protocol, excluding
file descriptor related messages.

Reference: <https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/sd_notify.html>

Usage:

```
import asyncio
import sd_notify

sdnotify = sd_notify.Notifier()

# Report a status message
await sdnotify.update_status("Initialising my service...")
await asyncio.sleep(3)

# Report that the program init is complete
await sdnotify.ready()
await sdnotify.update_status("Waiting for web requests...")
await asyncio.sleep(3)

# Report an error to the service manager
await sdnotify.set_watchdog_error("An irrecoverable error occured!")
```

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier._send
documentation:
Send a binary message via the notification socket.
If the `NOTIFY_SOCKET` environment variable is not set,
it will silently skip.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.ready
documentation: Report ready service state, i.e., completed initialization.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.stopping
documentation: Report the stopping/shutting-down service state.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.reloading
documentation: Report the reloading service state.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.set_errno
documentation: Set an errno-style integer code to indicate service failure.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.set_buserror
documentation: Set a D-Bus-style error code to indicate service failure.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.set_main_pid
documentation: Set the main PID for the case when the service manager did not fork the process itself.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.update_status
documentation: Set a custom service status message

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.keepalive
documentation:
Send a keepalive message to extend the watchdog timestamp.
If the time that this keepalive message is not sent to systemd exceeds the watchdog
timeout (WatchdogSec) then systemd will try to restart the service depending on
the service configuration.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.sd_notify.SystemdNotifier.trigger_watchdog
documentation:
Triggers the systemd's watchdog handler immediately.

If `msg` is specified, it will be reported as a custom status message to the
service manager to provide more information.

modules.name: ai.backend.common.validators
documentation: An extension module to Trafaret which provides additional type checkers.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.validators.StringLengthMeta
documentation: A metaclass that makes string-like trafarets to have sliced min/max length indicator.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.validators.AliasedKey
documentation:
An extension to trafaret.Key which accepts multiple aliases of a single key.
When successfully matched, the returned key name is the first one of the given aliases
or the renamed key set via `to_name()` method or the `>>` operator.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.validators.TimeDuration
documentation:
Represent the relative difference between two datetime objects,
parsed from human-readable time duration expression strings.

If you specify years or months, it returns an
{class}`dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta` instance
which keeps the human-friendly year and month calculation
considering leap years and monthly day count differences,
instead of simply multiplying 365 days to the value of years.
Otherwise, it returns the stdlib's {class}`datetime.timedelta`
instance.

Example:
\>>> t = datetime(2020, 2, 29)
\>>> t + check_and_return("1yr")
datetime.datetime(2021, 2, 28, 0, 0)
\>>> t + check_and_return("2yr")
datetime.datetime(2022, 2, 28, 0, 0)
\>>> t + check_and_return("3yr")
datetime.datetime(2023, 2, 28, 0, 0)
\>>> t + check_and_return("4yr")
datetime.datetime(2024, 2, 29, 0, 0) # preserves the same day of month

classes.name: ai.backend.common.validators.Delay
documentation:
Convert a float or a tuple of 2 floats into a random generated float value
to use in time.sleep() or asyncio.sleep()

modules.name: ai.backend.common.msgpack
documentation: Wrapper of msgpack-python with good defaults.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.events.AbstractEvent.serialize
documentation: Return a msgpack-serializable tuple.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.events.AbstractEvent.deserialize
documentation: Construct the event args from a tuple deserialized from msgpack.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.events.EventDispatcher

methods.name: ai.backend.common.events.EventDispatcher.consume
documentation:
Register a callback as a consumer. When multiple callback registers as a consumer
on a single event, only one callable among those will be called.

args_matcher:
  Optional. A callable which accepts event argument and supplies a bool as a return value.
  When specified, EventDispatcher will only execute callback when this lambda returns True.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.events.EventDispatcher.subscribe
documentation:
Subscribes to given event. All handlers will be called when certain event pops up.

args_matcher:
  Optional. A callable which accepts event argument and supplies a bool as a return value.
  When specified, EventDispatcher will only execute callback when this lambda returns True.

We have two types of event handlers: consumer and subscriber.

Consumers use the distribution pattern. Only one consumer among many manager worker processes
receives the event.

Consumer example: database updates upon specific events.

Subscribers use the broadcast pattern. All subscribers in many manager worker processes
receive the same event.

Subscriber example: enqueuing events to the queues for event streaming API handlers

classes.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.AbstractPlugin

methods.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.AbstractPlugin.__init__
documentation: Instantiate the plugin with the given initial configuration.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.AbstractPlugin.init
documentation: Initialize any resource used by the plugin.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.AbstractPlugin.cleanup
documentation: Clean up any resource used by the plugin upon server cleanup.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.AbstractPlugin.update_plugin_config
documentation:
Handle runtime configuration updates.
The config parameter contains both the updated parts
and unchanged parts of the configuration.

The default implementation is just to replace the config property,
but actual plugins may trigger other operations to reflect config changes
and/or inspect the differences of configs before replacing the current config.

class_variables.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.AbstractPlugin.plugin_config
documentation: ``plugin_config`` contains the plugin-specific configuration read from the etcd.

class_variables.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.AbstractPlugin.local_config
documentation:
``local_config`` contains the configuration read from the disk TOML file of the current daemon.
This configuration is only updated when restarting the daemon and thus plugins should assume
that it's read-only and immutable during its lifetime.
e.g., If the plugin is running with the manager, it's the validated content of manager.toml file.

class_variables.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.AbstractPlugin.config_watch_enabled
documentation:
If set True (default), the hosting plugin context will watch and automatically update
the etcd's plugin configuration changes via the `update_plugin_config()` method.
The minimum generic plugin interface.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.BasePluginContext
documentation:
A minimal plugin manager which controls the lifecycles of the given plugins
and watches & applies the configuration changes in etcd.

The subclasses must redefine `plugin_group`.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.hook.HookHandler
documentation:
The handler should accept a single argument containing
a tuple of parameters passed to the handler.
If it decides to cancel the ongoing event, it should raise
{class}`HookDenied` exception.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.hook.HookPlugin

methods.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.hook.HookPlugin.get_handlers
documentation:
Returns a sequence of pairs of the event name
and its corresponding handler function.
The abstract interface for hook plugins.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.hook.HookPluginContext

methods.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.hook.HookPluginContext.dispatch
documentation:
Invoke the handlers that matches with the given `event_name`.
If any of the handlers raises {class}`HookDenied`,
the event caller should seize the processing.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.plugin.hook.HookPluginContext.notify
documentation:
Invoke the handlers that matches with the given `event_name`.
Regardless of the handler results, the processing continues.
A manager for hook plugins with convenient handler invocation.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.asyncio.cancel_tasks
documentation:
Cancel all unfinished tasks from the given collection of asyncio tasks,
using {func}`asyncio.gather()` to let them clean up concurrently.
It returns the results and exceptions without raising them, for cases when
the caller wants to silent ignore errors or handle them at once.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.asyncio.run_through
documentation:
A syntactic sugar to simplify the code patterns like:

```python3
try:
    await do1()
except MyError:
    pass
try:
    await do2()
except MyError:
    pass
try:
    await do3()
except MyError:
    pass
```

Using `run_through()`, it becomes:

```python3
await run_through(
    do1(),
    do2(),
    do3(),
    ignored_exceptions=(MyError,),
)
```

variables.name: ai.backend.common.asyncio.current_loop
documentation: type: ignore

variables.name: ai.backend.common.asyncio.current_loop
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: ai.backend.common.asyncio.AsyncBarrier
documentation: This class provides a simplified asyncio-version of threading.Barrier class.

modules.name: ai.backend.common.logging
documentation: Kept for backward-compatibility of the plugins relying on the old common.logging module.

modules.name: ai.backend.common.cgroup
documentation: Relevant Linux kernel documentations
For /proc filesystem, see
https://docs.kernel.org/filesystems/proc.html
For cgroup v1, see
https://docs.kernel.org/admin-guide/cgroup-v1/
For cgroup v2, see
https://docs.kernel.org/admin-guide/cgroup-v2.html

modules.name: ai.backend.common.etcd_etcetra
documentation:
An asynchronous client wrapper for etcd v3 API.

It uses the etcd3 library using a thread pool executor.
We plan to migrate to aioetcd3 library but it requires more work to get maturity.
Fortunately, etcd3's watchers are not blocking because they are implemented
using callbacks in separate threads.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.etcd_etcetra.AsyncEtcd._merge_scope_prefix_map
documentation:
This stub ensures immutable usage of the ChainMap because ChainMap does *not*
have the immutable version in typeshed.
(ref: https://github.com/python/typeshed/issues/6042)

methods.name: ai.backend.common.etcd_etcetra.AsyncEtcd.put
documentation:
Put a single key-value pair to the etcd.

```
param key

The key. This must be quoted by the caller as needed.

param val

The value.

param scope

The config scope for putting the values.

param scope_prefix_map

The scope map used to mangle the prefix for the config scope.

return

```

methods.name: ai.backend.common.etcd_etcetra.AsyncEtcd.put_prefix
documentation:
Put a nested dict object under the given key prefix.
All keys in the dict object are automatically quoted to avoid conflicts with the path separator.

```
param key

Prefix to put the given data. This must be quoted by the caller as needed.

param dict_obj

Nested dictionary representing the data.

param scope

The config scope for putting the values.

param scope_prefix_map

The scope map used to mangle the prefix for the config scope.

return

```

methods.name: ai.backend.common.etcd_etcetra.AsyncEtcd.put_dict
documentation:
Put a flattened key-value pairs into the etcd.
Since the given dict must be a flattened one, its keys must be quoted as needed by the caller.
For new codes, `put_prefix()` is recommended.

```
param dict_obj

Flattened key-value pairs to put.

param scope

The config scope for putting the values.

param scope_prefix_map

The scope map used to mangle the prefix for the config scope.

return

```

methods.name: ai.backend.common.etcd_etcetra.AsyncEtcd.get
documentation:
Get a single key from the etcd.
Returns `None` if the key does not exist.
The returned value may be an empty string if the value is a zero-length string.

```
param key

The key. This must be quoted by the caller as needed.

param scope

The config scope to get the value.

param scope_prefix_map

The scope map used to mangle the prefix for the config scope.

return

```

methods.name: ai.backend.common.etcd_etcetra.AsyncEtcd.get_prefix
documentation:
Retrieves all key-value pairs under the given key prefix as a nested dictionary.
All dictionary keys are automatically unquoted.
If a key has a value while it is also used as path prefix for other keys,
the value directly referenced by the key itself is included as a value in a dictionary
with the empty-string key.

For instance, when the etcd database has the following key-value pairs:

```
myprefix/mydata = abc
myprefix/mydata/x = 1
myprefix/mydata/y = 2
myprefix/mykey = def
```

`get_prefix("myprefix")` returns the following dictionary:

```
{
  "mydata": {
    "": "abc",
    "x": "1",
    "y": "2",
  },
  "mykey": "def",
}
```

```
param key_prefix

The key. This must be quoted by the caller as needed.

param scope

The config scope to get the value.

param scope_prefix_map

The scope map used to mangle the prefix for the config scope.

return

```

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.UnknownImageReference
documentation:
Represents an error for invalid/unknown image reference.
The first argument of this exception should be the reference given by the user.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.ImageNotAvailable
documentation:
Represents an error for unavailability of the image in agents.
The first argument of this exception should be the reference given by the user.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.UnknownImageRegistry
documentation:
Represents an error for invalid/unknown image registry.
The first argument of this exception should be the registry given by the user.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.InvalidImageName
documentation: Represents an invalid string for image name.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.InvalidImageTag
documentation:
Represents an invalid string for image tag and full image name.
Image tag should be a string of form below

```
<version-stringA>-<platform-tag-1A>-<platform-tag-2A>-....
```

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.ProjectMismatchWithCanonical
documentation: Represent the project value does not match the canonical value when parsing the string representing the image in ImageRef.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.AliasResolutionFailed
documentation:
Represents an alias resolution failure.
The first argument of this exception should be the alias given by the user.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.InvalidIpAddressValue
documentation: Represents an invalid value for ip_address.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.VolumeMountFailed
documentation: Represents a mount process failure.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.exception.VolumeUnmountFailed
documentation: Represents a umount process failure.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.cli.LazyClickMixin
documentation:
Click's documentations says "supports lazy loading of subcommands at runtime",
but there is no actual examples and how-tos as indicated by the issue:
https://github.com/pallets/click/issues/945

This class fills the gap by binding the methods of original Click classes to
a wrapper that lazily loads the underlying Click object.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.events_experimental.read_stream
documentation: A high-level wrapper for the XREAD command.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.distributed.GlobalTimer
documentation:
Executes the given async function only once in the given interval,
uniquely among multiple manager instances across multiple nodes.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.redis_client.RedisClient._send
documentation:
Executes a function that issues Redis commands or returns a pipeline/transaction of commands,
with automatic retries upon temporary connection failures.

Note that when retried, the given function may be executed *multiple* times, so the caller
should take care of side-effects of it.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.bgtask.BackgroundTaskManager.push_bgtask_events

A aiohttp-based server-sent events (SSE) responder that pushes the bgtask updates
to the clients.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.bgtask.BackgroundTaskManager.poll_bgtask_event
documentation:
RHS of return tuple will be filled with extra informations when needed
(e.g. progress information of task when callee is trying to poll information of already completed one)

functions.name: ai.backend.common.networking.curl
documentation:
A simple curl-like helper function that uses aiohttp to fetch some string/data
from a remote HTTP endpoint.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.networking.find_free_port
documentation:
Find a freely available TCP port in the current host.
Note that since under certain conditions this may have races.

modules.name: ai.backend.common.logging_utils
documentation: Kept for backward-compatibility of the plugins relying on the old common.logging_utils module.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.redis_helper.subscribe
documentation:
An async-generator wrapper for pub-sub channel subscription.
It automatically recovers from server shutdowns until explicitly cancelled.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.redis_helper.blpop
documentation:
An async-generator wrapper for blpop (blocking left pop).
It automatically recovers from server shutdowns until explicitly cancelled.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.redis_helper.execute
documentation:
Executes a function that issues Redis commands or returns a pipeline/transaction of commands,
with automatic retries upon temporary connection failures.

Note that when retried, the given function may be executed *multiple* times, so the caller
should take care of side-effects of it.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.redis_helper.execute_script
documentation:
Auto-load and execute the given script.
It uses the hash keys for scripts so that it does not send the whole
script every time but only at the first time.

Args:
    conn: A Redis connection or pool with the commands mixin.
    script_id: A human-readable identifier for the script.
        This can be arbitrary string but must be unique for each script.
    script: The script content.
    keys: The Redis keys that will be passed to the script.
    args: The arguments that will be passed to the script.

functions.name: ai.backend.common.types._stringify_number
documentation: Stringify a number, preventing unwanted scientific notations.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.types.aobject

methods.name: ai.backend.common.types.aobject.new
documentation:
We can do `await SomeAObject(...)`, but this makes mypy
to complain about its return type with `await` statement.
This is a copy of `__new__()` to workaround it.

methods.name: ai.backend.common.types.aobject.__ainit__
documentation:
Automatically called when creating the instance using
`await SubclassOfAObject(...)`
where the arguments are passed to `__init__()` as in
the vanilla Python classes.

An "asynchronous" object which guarantees to invoke both `def __init__(self, ...)` and
`async def __ainit(self)__` to ensure asynchronous initialization of the object.

You can create an instance of subclasses of aboject in the following way:

```python
o = await SomeAObj.new(...)
```

Convert wild-card based IP address into CIDR.

e.g)
192.10.*.* -> 192.10.0.0/16

classes.name: ai.backend.common.types.BinarySize
documentation:
A wrapper around Python integers to represent binary sizes for storage and
memory in various places.

Its string representation and parser, `from_str()` classmethod, does not use
any locale-specific digit delimeters -- it supports only standard Python
digit delimeters.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.types.ResourceSlot

value: `str` or `Decimal` type value. Do not convert this to `float` or `int`.

classes.name: ai.backend.common.types.VFolderUsageMode
documentation:
Usage mode of virtual folder.

GENERAL: normal virtual folder
MODEL: virtual folder which provides shared models
DATA: virtual folder which provides shared data
